applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile with mode=default
applied_optimizations.description: Applied torch.compile(mode='default') to the entire ModelNew class. This enables torch inductor to fuse elementwise operations (LeakyReLU, Multiply, MaxPool3d) and optimize the computation graph. Achieved 1.38x speedup (0.925ms -> 0.669ms).

attempted_but_failed.name: Custom Triton kernels for elementwise ops
attempted_but_failed.reason: Custom Triton kernels for LeakyReLU-Multiply-LeakyReLU and MaxPool3d were actually slower than PyTorch's native implementations. The torch.compile inductor already fuses these operations efficiently.
attempted_but_failed.error: Custom kernels achieved 10.3ms vs 0.922ms reference (0.09x speedup)

notes: torch.compile(mode='default') is the key optimization - gives 1.34x speedup out of the box
Custom Triton kernels for simple elementwise ops are NOT beneficial here - inductor already fuses them
Main bottleneck is ConvTranspose3d (61.7% of GPU time) - split between memory transpose (34.6%) and compute (27.1%)
Further optimization would require optimizing the convolution itself, but that's handled by MIOpen/rocBLAS
The fused Triton kernel generated by inductor (triton_poi_fused_convolution_leaky_relu_mul_0) is already efficient

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trials 2, 3, and 4 all produced no output — the agent is repeatedly getting stuck. Need to provide an extremely explicit, copy-paste-ready solution so the agent can succeed on this last stage.

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 5 (and trials 2-4) all failed with no output. The agent keeps getting stuck. Providing an extremely explicit, copy-paste-ready solution using channels_last_3d + torch.compile to eliminate the 34

global_notes: For this problem, torch.compile is the winning strategy
The problem involves ConvTranspose3d which is already highly optimized in MIOpen
Elementwise fusion via torch.compile inductor is sufficient - no need for manual Triton kernels
